description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_enumeration, mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Deliverable:
Add `test/mutagen_ex/determinism_test.exs` (new file, tagged `:e2e_slow`). The test runs `MutagenEx.run/2` twice against a small bench fixture and asserts the produced NDJSON output is byte-identical across runs.

The fixture lives at `priv/helper_scripts/bench_fixtures/wrd25_200sites/` (added in S1). Until S1 lands, the test can target an existing tiny scope as the input â€” the goal is to commit the safety net before any refactor begins. Re-target to the wrd25 fixture in S1.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test --include e2e_slow test/mutagen_ex/determinism_test.exs` green
- Behaviors to demonstrate: two consecutive `MutagenEx.run/2` invocations against the same input produce binary-identical NDJSON output (the mutagen.r2 determinism contract)

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/**/* (no production code changes)
- priv/repo/migrations/** (none exist; defensive)
Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- test/mutagen_ex/determinism_test.exs (new)

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not refactor anything. This is the safety-net commit before refactoring begins.
- Do not add a fixture under priv/helper_scripts/ â€” S1 owns that.
- Do not include in default `mix test` (must be `:e2e_slow` tagged).

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (`mix compile --warnings-as-errors`)
- [ ] `mix test` (default) still green (the new test is excluded by default)
- [ ] `mix test --include e2e_slow test/mutagen_ex/determinism_test.exs` green
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Commit: `mutagen-wrd.25.00: pre-flight determinism test`

Tests added:
- MutagenEx.DeterminismTest "two runs of mix mutagen against arith.ex produce byte-identical iodata"
  (single test under describe "deterministic JSON output across consecutive runs (mutagen.r2 contract)")

Verification stubs flipped: none
  The ticket's advanced subjects (mutagen.mutation_enumeration, mutagen.mutation_pipeline) already
  carry their own spec-verification entries (all execute: true). The new test does not have its own
  spec-verification stub â€” S0 is a pre-flight safety net that anchors the r15 determinism contract
  before the wrd25 refactor begins; it lives directly in the test tree under the :e2e_slow tag.

Summary:
Adds test/mutagen_ex/determinism_test.exs which runs Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.run/2 twice against
lib/lane_fixture/arith.ex with --seed 0 and --timeout-ms 2000, captures the emitted JSON iodata
via a stub IO collaborator, and asserts the two raw binaries are byte-identical. The harness
mirrors EndToEndTest's collaborator-dispatch pattern (CaptureIo / Baseline / Coverage stubs +
lane-fixture beam recompile via setup_all + per-run cover.stop + beam reload state reset) but is
self-contained so future refactors of EndToEndTest cannot accidentally drop the determinism
safety net. The test is tagged :e2e_slow so it stays out of default `mix test`. Per the ticket,
S1 will re-target this test at priv/helper_scripts/bench_fixtures/wrd25_200sites/ once that
fixture lands; the contract being asserted (byte-equality across two consecutive runs) does
not change with the retarget.

Note on entry point: the ticket text names MutagenEx.run/2, but the actual public entry on the
codebase today is Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.run/2 (no top-level lib/mutagen_ex.ex). I used the actual
entry. The contract â€” byte-identical NDJSON across two consecutive invocations against the same
input â€” is unchanged.

Verification:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors: green
- mix test (default): 517 tests, 0 failures, 16 excluded (new file excluded by :e2e_slow tag)
- mix test --include e2e_slow test/mutagen_ex/determinism_test.exs: 1 test, 0 failures

Semantic checks:

- Requirements coverage (S0 is a cross-cutting safety net; per-requirement coverage was already established by the v1-v7 stubs that exist on the Advances subjects â€” S0 adds ONE additional property that those stubs do not assert):
  - mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r15 (byte-identical aggregate across two runs of the same cfg): covered by test/mutagen_ex/determinism_test.exs:103 "two runs of mix mutagen against arith.ex produce byte-identical iodata". Would fail if any future refactor (S1-S6 AST-lift / batched-prewalk / cache-extension) introduces non-determinism â€” e.g. unordered map iteration in result serialisation, time-based field, async-stream collision, or seed leakage â€” the assertion `bin1 == bin2` catches the first divergence and the helper `first_diff_window/2` localises the byte offset.
  - All other r-IDs on mutation_pipeline and mutation_enumeration (r1-r14, e1-e8) remain covered by pre-existing tests under the existing v1-v7 stubs, which were not modified by this commit and continue to pass.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none (no lib changes, no fixture under priv/helper_scripts/, test correctly e2e_slow-tagged so default `mix test` does not run it)
- Project verification:
  - mix compile --warnings-as-errors: green (exit 0)

  - mix test --include e2e_slow test/mutagen_ex/determinism_test.exs: 1/1, 0 failures
- Audit-expanded scope: none

Source-claim trace:
- The test invokes Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.run/2 with a dispatch map of behaviour-backed collaborators (CaptureIoCollaborator implements MutagenEx.Pipeline.IoFacade; BaselineCollaborator implements MutagenEx.Pipeline.BaselineFacade; CoverageCollaborator implements MutagenEx.Pipeline.CoverageFacade). The implementer's note about MutagenEx.run/2 vs Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.run/2 is correct â€” there is no top-level lib/mutagen_ex.ex public entry; using the Mix task entry honours the contract (byte-identical NDJSON across two consecutive invocations against the same input).
- The test pins --seed 0 and --timeout-ms 2000, mirroring decisions/serial_execution_and_seed.md (default seed 0, max_cases: 1 forced).
- Per-run state reset (:cover.stop + beam purge/reload + capture-target ProcessDict cleanup) matches EndToEndTest's reset_e2e_state!/1 recipe; both runs start from equivalent Code.Server / cover / ExUnit state, so any drift in `bin1` vs `bin2` is genuinely a determinism contract break and not a state-carryover artefact.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- The S0 ticket explicitly defers fixture creation (priv/helper_scripts/bench_fixtures/wrd25_200sites/) to S1; this test currently targets the existing lane_project arith.ex scope, which the ticket text explicitly authorises ("the test can target an existing tiny scope as the input"). S1 will re-target without changing the contract.
- The captured stdout during the test run shows ExUnit failure dumps for LaneFixture.ArithTest tests â€” these are the INTERNAL mutation runs the pipeline executes (mutated arith.ex code raises `ArithmeticError` / produces wrong results, killing the mutations). These are expected output of the harness under test, not failures of the determinism test itself; the outer test result is "1 test, 0 failures".
- No new spec-verification stubs were added by this commit. The implementer's framing (S0 is a safety net anchored on r15 rather than its own stub-bearing addition) matches the ticket's "Allowed touches" list which only authorises the test file. Should the team later want this asserted as a tracked spec-verification entry, that would be a follow-on edit to mutation_pipeline.spec.md under a separate ticket.

AUDIT: APPROVE

Reasoning:
- Scope mechanical re-check (independent of verifier): `git diff 792acea..HEAD --name-status` = exactly one `A test/mutagen_ex/determinism_test.exs` (+388 lines). Zero touches to `lib/**`, `.spec/**`, `priv/**`, `mix.exs`, `mix.lock`. Matches Allowed touches exactly; no Out-of-Scope file violations; no Out-of-Scope-intent violations (no refactor, no `priv/helper_scripts/bench_fixtures/wrd25_200sites/` fixture, `:e2e_slow` tag excludes it from default `mix test` â€” empirically confirmed: `mix test test/mutagen_ex/determinism_test.exs` reports "All tests have been excluded").
- r15 coverage: the assertion `bin1 == bin2` (test/mutagen_ex/determinism_test.exs:125) over two captured `Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.run/2` invocations is genuinely load-bearing. The dispatch overrides only `io`/`tests`/`baseline`/`coverage`/`mutation`; `BaselineCollaborator.run/1` (line 225) and `CoverageCollaborator.run/1` (line 236) DELEGATE to `MutagenEx.Baseline.run/1` and `MutagenEx.CoverageRunner.run/1` respectively, and `mutation: MutagenEx.MutationRunner` is the real production module. So the real enumerationâ†’baselineâ†’coverageâ†’mutationâ†’JsonReporter path runs end-to-end; any nondeterminism (unordered map iteration in serialisation, time-based field, seed leakage, async-stream collision) would surface as a byte diff and the helper `first_diff_window/2` localises the offset. This complements v7 at `test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_parallel_test.exs:177` (which tests parallel/serial-equivalence with synthetic sites) by adding the missing integration-level guard.
- State reset between the two runs (`:cover.stop` + beam purge/reload + ProcessDict cleanup, lines 261-286) mirrors `EndToEndTest.reset_e2e_state!/1`, so a `bin1 != bin2` divergence is genuinely a determinism contract break â€” not a state-carryover artefact. The 300s receive timeout is generous; the test ran in ~0.4s end-to-end against the arith scope.
- Numbers cross-checked empirically: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` green; `mix test` default = 517 tests, 0 failures, 16 excluded; `mix test --include e2e_slow test/mutagen_ex/determinism_test.exs` = 1 test, 0 failures. All three match the verifier's and implementer's claims exactly. The captured "5 tests, 1 failure" lines in the test stdout are the internal mutation runs (mutations killing the cited test) â€” outer test result remains 1/0, as the verifier correctly identified.
- Entry-point substitution (`MutagenEx.run/2` â†’ `Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.run/2`) is correct: no `lib/mutagen_ex.ex` exists; the Mix task at lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:256 is the actual public entry. Contract preserved.
- Spec-stub framing: implementer correctly did not flip a new spec-verification stub for S0 (the Advances subjects' v1-v7 stubs remain untouched and continue to pass, including v7 at line 515 which already covers r15 at the runner-unit layer). S0 is an additional integration-level safety net per the ticket's "Allowed touches" â€” adding a stub would have required editing `.spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md`, which is out of scope. Reasonable call.

No rubber-stamping or scope miss detected. Verifier's `Would fail if â€¦` reasoning is genuinely load-bearing for the r15 literal contract (byte-identical aggregate across two runs of the same cfg).
APPROVED. Merged into epic/mutagen-wrd (fast-forward, post-merge smoke green 517/0/16-excluded). Closing per orchestrator sign-off.
